summary: Yash Rathi is currently at India Quotient with prior experience at two other VC firms (Titan Capital, 3one4 Capital) and a founder's office role at Adiabatic. His LinkedIn profile shows a career trajectory focused on venture capital and investing rather than operating or founding companies, with educational background in Electronics & Instrumentation Engineering from BITS Pilani Goa.

result.summary: Yash Rathi is currently at India Quotient with prior experience at two other VC firms (Titan Capital, 3one4 Capital) and a founder's office role at Adiabatic. His LinkedIn profile shows a career trajectory focused on venture capital and investing rather than operating or founding companies, with educational background in Electronics & Instrumentation Engineering from BITS Pilani Goa.

career_signal.reasoning: Has worked at three credible VC firms (India Quotient, Titan Capital, 3one4 Capital) and one founder's office (Adiabatic), showing exposure to the startup ecosystem. However, the profile lacks specifics on role duration, responsibilities, or achievements at these firms.

founder_relevance.reasoning: No evidence of founding, building, or shipping products. All listed roles are investor-side (VC firms) or support roles (founder's office). The profile suggests observer/investor orientation rather than builder/operator experience.

execution_signal.reasoning: No concrete evidence of shipping products, managing teams, hitting KPIs, or delivering measurable outcomes. The profile mentions interests and passion but provides no specific achievements, projects built, or execution milestones at any role.

credibility_signal.reasoning: BITS Pilani Goa is a strong engineering institution. Experience at reputable VC firms (Titan Capital, 3one4 Capital, India Quotient) adds credibility. CFA Level 1 cleared and TEDx/HPAIR/180 DC mentions show initiative. However, lacks depth on what was accomplished at these positions.

strengths: Strong educational pedigree from BITS Pilani Goa with Engineering + Finance background
Exposure to multiple credible VC firms (Titan Capital, 3one4 Capital, India Quotient)
CFA Level 1 cleared shows financial acumen and discipline
Large network (8,667 connections, 9,029 followers) suggests strong relationship-building
Exposure to founder's office at Adiabatic provides some operating context
concerns: No evidence of founding, building, or operating a company—entire career is investor-side
Profile lacks specifics on role durations, responsibilities, deal involvement, or achievements
No demonstrated execution signal—nothing shipped, built, or delivered
Career trajectory suggests analyst/investor rather than operator/founder archetype
Profile reads more like an aspiring VC professional than a founder seeking capital
missing_information: Why is someone currently at India Quotient being evaluated as a founder? Is this a side project or transition?
Any actual founding or building experience outside of VC roles
Specific tenure and accomplishments at each VC firm
What problem is being solved and what company is being pitched
Any concrete evidence of technical building, product shipping, or operational experience
Details on the Adiabatic founder's office role—scope, duration, outcomes
next_questions_for_diligence: What is the venture you're founding and why are you transitioning from VC to operating?
Can you describe something specific you've built, shipped, or executed end-to-end outside of investing work?
What lived experience or deep insight into this problem do you have beyond pattern recognition from VC?
During your time at Adiabatic's founder's office, what operational responsibilities did you own and what were the outcomes?
Who are your co-founders and what complementary operating/building experience do they bring?

summary: Gagan Goyal is a General Partner at India Quotient with entrepreneur-turned-investor credentials. He founded and successfully exited ThinkLABS, a STEM/robotics ed-tech company in 2016, and has since built an active angel portfolio of 20+ startups including Housing.com, Fynd, and Chaayos. IIT Bombay graduate with multiple recognitions as a young entrepreneur.

result.summary: Gagan Goyal is a General Partner at India Quotient with entrepreneur-turned-investor credentials. He founded and successfully exited ThinkLABS, a STEM/robotics ed-tech company in 2016, and has since built an active angel portfolio of 20+ startups including Housing.com, Fynd, and Chaayos. IIT Bombay graduate with multiple recognitions as a young entrepreneur.

career_signal.reasoning: Strong progression from IIT Bombay (B.Tech Mechanical) to founding ThinkLABS, achieving successful exit in 2016, then transitioning to GP at India Quotient (2017–present). Active angel investor with 20+ portfolio companies and multiple exits demonstrates sustained career momentum in the startup ecosystem.

founder_relevance.reasoning: Founded ThinkLABS (STEM/robotics ed-tech) and built it to successful exit. Profile emphasizes 'entrepreneur-turned-investor' identity and 'building a category-defining business from scratch.' Winner of Eureka 2004 national business plan competition at IIT Bombay. Active angel investor shows continued founder engagement.

execution_signal.reasoning: Successfully exited ThinkLABS after building 'IP-driven culture that transformed hands-on learning for students across India.' As angel investor, secured 'multiple high-impact exits' from 20+ portfolio companies. However, specific metrics (revenue, users, scale) and timeline details are absent from profile.

credibility_signal.reasoning: IIT Bombay graduate, featured in India Today '35 Young Achievers' cover story, Top 10 Young Entrepreneurs (Business World India), Top 25 Young Entrepreneurs in Asia (Business Week USA). Portfolio includes notable startups (Housing.com, Fynd, Chaayos). Involved in FIRST Global robotics Olympics since 2016 and established STEM Education Trust.

strengths: Proven founder with successful exit of ThinkLABS in ed-tech/STEM space, demonstrating ability to build and scale a business to exit
Deep India/Bharat insight through ed-tech venture that 'transformed hands-on learning for students across India' and philanthropy focused on underserved backgrounds
Strong credibility signals: IIT Bombay, multiple national/international entrepreneurship awards, featured in major publications
Active angel investor track record with 20+ startups and multiple exits, showing deal-sourcing ability and network
Currently GP at India Quotient (2017-present) with stated focus on pre-seed/seed across consumer hardware, SaaS, fintech, ed-tech
concerns: Profile lacks specific metrics about ThinkLABS (revenue, growth rate, team size, number of students impacted, exit details)
No information about how long ThinkLABS was operated before exit or scale achieved
Angel investment success rate and exit multiples not quantified - 'multiple high-impact exits' is vague
Profile is written from investor perspective rather than founder/operator perspective, making it harder to assess depth of operational experience
missing_information: ThinkLABS founding year, growth trajectory, revenue/unit economics, team size, and exit valuation/acquirer
Specific roles/responsibilities between IIT Bombay graduation and ThinkLABS founding
Details on which angel investments had successful exits and returns generated
Concrete examples of 'IP-driven culture' and specific innovations/patents at ThinkLABS
Current fund size, investment thesis details, and portfolio performance at India Quotient
next_questions_for_diligence: Walk us through the ThinkLABS journey: when did you found it, what traction did you achieve (revenue, users, schools), and what were the exit terms?
What was the hardest operational challenge you faced at ThinkLABS and how did you solve it? Can you share specific metrics before/after?
Of your 20+ angel investments, which 2-3 are you most proud of and why? What was your value-add beyond capital?
You mention 'frugal innovation' - give us an example from ThinkLABS where resource constraints led to a creative breakthrough.
What specific founder-market fit or lived experience do you bring that makes you credible in your current investment focus areas (consumer hardware, SaaS, fintech, ed-tech)?

profile.summary:
General Partner at India Quotient, where I focus on identifying and scaling early-stage startups that solve deep-rooted problems in India through technology and frugal innovation. As an entrepreneur-turned-investor, I am passionate about supporting founders as a "buddy" who understands the roller-coaster journey of building a category-defining business from scratch. 

Venture Capital Leadership (2017–Present): I specialise in pre-seed and seed-stage opportunities across consumer hardware, SaaS, fintech, and ed-tech, prioritising product-market fit over vanity metrics.

Entrepreneurial Exit (2016): Successfully exited ThinkLABS, a pioneering STEM and robotics ed-tech company I founded. Built a high-energy, IP-driven culture that transformed hands-on learning for students across India.

Strategic Angel Investing: Active early-stage investor with a portfolio of over 20 startups, including notable names like Housing.com, Fynd, Chaayos, and Zeno Health, securing multiple high-impact exits.

Philanthropy & STEM Education: Deeply involved in the "Olympics of Robotics" via FIRST Global since 2016. Established the STEM Education Trust to mentor talented students from underserved backgrounds, helping them compete and win on the world stage. 

I am an IIT Bombay graduate driven by the mission to make the Indian startup ecosystem more efficient, sane, and impactful.
profile.headline: Pre-seed & Seed stage Investor

position.title: Co-founder & CEO
position.description:
Founded ThinkLABS in 2006 with the objective of "promoting hands-on learning & STEM education" so that learning process can be much for engaging and effective which is predominantly a rote-based learning since long. 

I have been through the roller-coaster journey of conceiving the idea to creating a new business category in Indian education market. Have seen challenges of product market fit to sales to raising capital. 

Finally built a sustainable company, got a cash exit by selling it to strategic players. This would not have been possible without our highly motivated team, early stage investors and supporters who always believed in us.

fullPositions.title: Co-founder & CEO

multiLocaleDescription.en_US: FIRST Global (www.first.global) - The Olympics of Robotics - A new initiative by Dean Kamen (Inventor of Segway and founder of FIRST) - to foster the interest and recognition of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) skills among young students. It's a Global championship that combines the excitement of sports with the rigour of science and technology.

volunteering.description: FIRST Global (www.first.global) - The Olympics of Robotics - A new initiative by Dean Kamen (Inventor of Segway and founder of FIRST) - to foster the interest and recognition of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) skills among young students. It's a Global championship that combines the excitement of sports with the rigour of science and technology.

volunteering.title: Trustee

multiLocaleDescription.en_US: "Co-founder of STEM Education Trust, a non-profit dedicated to making robotics 'the next great equalizer' in Indian education. As the official India partner for FIRST Global since 2017, we identify and mentor talented students from underserved backgrounds, empowering them to represent India on the world stage of innovation. Our mission is to bridge the gap between classroom theory and real-world problem solving through hands-on STEM experiences."
